title: How Ian Goodfellow Invented GANs - DeepLearning.AI
excerpts:
Section Title: Ian Goodfellow A Man, A Plan, A GAN
Content:
Brilliant ideas strike at unlikely moments. [Ian Goodfellow](https://www.deeplearning.ai/blog/hodl-ian-goodfellow/) conceived generative adversarial networks while spitballing programming techniques with friends at a bar. Goodfellow, who views himself as “someone who works on the core technology, not the applications,” started at Stanford as a premed before switching to computer science and studying machine learning with Andrew Ng. “I realized that would be a faster path to impact more things than working on specific medical applications one at a time,” he recalls. From there, he earned a PhD in machine learning at Université de Montréal, interned at the seminal robotics lab Willow Garage, and held positions at OpenAI and Google Research. Last year, he joined Apple as director of machine learning in the Special Projects Group, which develops technologies that aren’t part of products on the market. His work at Apple is top-secr

title: Ian Goodfellow on Inventing GANs - Editorial - Le Random
excerpts:
Section Title: **Ian Goodfellow on Inventing GANs**
Content:
*In this written form of the conversation, deep learning engineer and generative adversarial network (GAN) inventor Ian Goodfellow speaks with Peter Bauman (* [*Monk Antony*](https://x.com/monkantony_tez) *) in depth about the legendary origins of GANs, their unexpected success and indelible impact on both twenty-first-century image making and AI research. The full audio form of the conversation is now available* [*on*](https://open.spotify.com/episode/116hKZempQsL20gnoWMCS6) [*podcast*](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/31-ian-goodfellow-on-inventing-gans/id1726153340?i=1000735635188) *and* [*YouTube*](https://youtu.be/p-pBWXLlvfI) *.* ‍ **
Peter Bauman: GANs have played an outsized, enormously significant role in the development of deep generative models, especially in the transition from tasks like feature learning to actual synthesis and generation.** ‍
‍ **In your own words, can you explain what GANs are and what the

title: Ian  Goodfellow | RSA Conference
excerpts:
Section Title: Ian Goodfellow
Content:
Staff Research Scientist, Google Brain
Biography Past Contributions 2 Presentations 2
Ian Goodfellow is a Staff Research Scientist. He leads a research group in Google Brain studying adversarial techniques in AI. He is the Lead Author of the first major textbook on deep learning— *Deep Learning* (MIT Press). With his collaborators at Google, he published some of the first research on security and privacy of deep learning.
[View Ian's Profile on Twitter](https://www.twitter.com/@goodfellow_ian)
PRESENTATION
April 19, 2018

title: Ian Goodfellow - Wikipedia
excerpts: **Ian J. Goodfellow** (born 1987 <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> ) is an American computer scientist , engineer , and executive , most noted for his work on artificial neural networks and deep learning . He is a research scientist at Google DeepMind , <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> was previously employed as a research scientist at Google Brain and director of machine learning at Apple as well as one of the first employees at OpenAI , and has made several important contributions to the field of deep learning , including the invention of the generative adversarial network (GAN). Goodfellow co-wrote, as the first author, the textbook *Deep Learning* (2016) <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> and wrote the chapter on deep learning in the authoritative textbook of the field of artificial intelligence, *Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach* <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> (used in more than 1,500 universities in 135 countries). <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup>

title: GitHub - f0nzie/transcript_interview_ian_goodfellow_by_lex_fridman
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Ian Goodfellow: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) | MIT Artificial Intelligence (AI) P...
Content:
This is a quick transcript of the interview of Ian Goodfellow by [Lex Fridman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lexfridman) . The interview has been published in [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z6rxFNMGdn0) as well as a podcast. Goodfellow points at so many areas in the future where ML and AI could bring astonishing discoveries. Enjoy! *Alfonso R. Reyes. The Woodlands, Texas.*

title: Ian Goodfellow - Research at Google
excerpts:
Section Title: Ian Goodfellow
Content:
Ian Goodfellow (PhD in machine learning, University of Montreal, 2014) is a research
scientist at Google. His research interests include most deep learning topics,
especially generative models and machine learning security and privacy. He invented
generative adversarial networks, was an influential early researcher studying
adversarial examples, and is the lead author of the MIT Press textbook Deep Learning
(www.deeplearningbook.org). He runs the Self-Organizing Conference on Machine
Learning, which was founded at OpenAI in 2016. More information is available at
www.iangoodfellow.com
